context.project_description: Implement token usage tracking for LLM calls to enable monitoring of query costs by counting tokens consumed per operation across different provider types (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama).
key_requirements: Create a benchmark hook that tracks tokens per LLM call and integrates with existing instrumentation framework
Implement instrumented provider wrappers that wrap core LLM provider types to add token counting functionality
Add data model for representing token usage metrics per operation
Instrument both streaming and non-streaming cases appropriately
constraints: Leverage existing BenchmarkHook pattern and InstrumentedProxy system rather than building new monitoring infrastructure
Ensure token tracking works across all supported LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Handle both streaming and non-streaming API cases with appropriate token accounting methods
Do not duplicate or modify existing provider implementations directly
stakeholders: user requesting token usage tracking to see how many LLM tokens each query costs

in_scope: tracking input/output token counts for chat and embedding operations
integration with existing instrumentation framework using BenchmarkHook pattern
support for multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
out_of_scope: token tracking in streaming cases for MVP
modification of application code for token counting
creation or modification of fitz_ai/llm/config.py (file does not exist in codebase)

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls

assumptions.assumption: Token counting needs to be done at the API layer rather than in application code
assumptions.impact: Would need to modify how tokens are tracked (in SDK, query processing logic)

assumptions.assumption: The instrumentation system should leverage existing BenchmarkHook pattern
assumptions.impact: Would require building a new monitoring interface instead of using `InstrumentedProxy`

assumptions.assumption: Each LLM provider has distinct token counting logic that varies by model/endpoint
assumptions.impact: If all providers used same counting approach, could be simplified to one generic tracker

assumptions.assumption: Token tracking doesn't need to happen in the streaming case yet for MVP
assumptions.impact: Would require more complex integration for streaming responses (e.g., accumulating tokens over stream)

assumptions.assumption: Users care about per-query total cost, not individual operations within a query
assumptions.impact: If they need operation-by-operation breakdown, we'd need to track each call separately and aggregate

approaches.name: Instrumented Provider Wrappers with Context Storage
approaches.description: Wrap LLM providers with instrumentation that stores raw API responses in thread-local storage for token extraction by hooks, maintaining existing provider interfaces while enabling token tracking.
pros: Preserves all existing provider functionality and interfaces
Leverages existing instrumentation framework without major changes
Supports all currently supported providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Minimal impact on performance for non-tracked operations
cons: Requires thread-local storage modification to capture raw responses
Hook implementation must access context rather than direct return values
Complexity in maintaining state across async and concurrent calls
best_for: Systems requiring token tracking without interface modifications

approaches.name: Modified Provider Interfaces with Token Returns
approaches.description: Extend base LLM provider protocols to optionally return token usage alongside results, allowing hooks to directly access structured response data for token extraction.
pros: Clean separation of concerns and explicit contract definition
Simpler hook implementation that can directly access token data
Supports streaming operations if needed in future
Clear interface contracts for all LLM operations
cons: Breaking change to existing provider interfaces requires careful migration
Must update all provider implementations and consumers of those interfaces
Higher risk of compatibility issues across dependent components
best_for: Systems where token tracking is core requirement

architecture.recommended: Instrumented Provider Wrappers with Context Storage
architecture.reasoning: The Instrumented Provider Wrappers approach is recommended because the current analysis clearly identifies that the proposed implementation fails due to incompatible interface contracts - LLM providers return simple types like strings or lists, but token extraction requires access to structured API responses. The Instrumented Provider Wrappers approach leverages existing instrumentation patterns while working around this limitation by storing raw response data for hooks to later extract tokens from. This is more practical than modifying provider interfaces which would be a breaking change requiring extensive codebase updates. However, the analysis also correctly identifies that both approaches are flawed due to fundamental design issues with how token data flows through the system - specifically that providers convert responses to simple types before instrumentation can access them for token extraction.

key_tradeoffs.feature_scope: Limiting token tracking to non-streaming operations (chat, embed) rather than implementing streaming token counting to reduce complexity and focus on MVP requirements
key_tradeoffs.provider_coverage: Restricting instrumentation to only OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, and Ollama providers to achieve 95% usage coverage while avoiding scope creep
key_tradeoffs.error_handling: Choosing explicit failure with descriptive exceptions over silent error handling for token parsing issues to improve debugging and observability

technology_considerations: Python dataclasses for TokenUsage model to leverage built-in serialization and type safety
InstrumentedProxy system from fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py for consistent hook integration across providers
BenchmarkHook protocol for interception points in the call lifecycle
Thread-local storage or context passing mechanism needed to access raw response objects for token extraction (current implementation incomplete)
Provider factory pattern in fitz_ai/llm/client.py must be extended to support instrumented wrappers
architecture.scope_statement: Implement token usage tracking via instrumentation wrappers around existing LLM providers while preserving backward compatibility. This requires extending thread-local storage mechanisms and updating hook implementations to extract tokens from raw API responses rather than converted return values.

adrs.title: ADR: Token Usage Tracking for LLM Providers
adrs.context: Need to track token usage across different LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) to understand cost and performance implications of queries. The system should provide visibility into prompt tokens, completion tokens, and total tokens consumed per operation.
adrs.decision: Implement a benchmark hook-based instrumentation system that wraps provider methods while maintaining backward compatibility with existing interfaces. Only implement token tracking for standard protocols from base.py, using the existing InstrumentedProxy infrastructure.
adrs.rationale: This approach leverages the existing instrumentation framework in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py which already supports intercepting method calls via BenchmarkHook protocol. It provides a clean separation of concerns - token tracking logic is separate from provider implementation and doesn't require changes to core LLM interfaces or breaking existing behavior.
consequences: Token usage data will be available through the TokenTrackingHook's token_usages property
Only providers that implement standard protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, etc.) will have token tracking
Streaming operations are not tracked in MVP due to complexity of handling incremental token counts
Providers must return structured response objects for tokens to be extracted (this limitation is a design constraint)
No changes needed to the core provider interfaces or breaking existing API contracts
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Modify all LLM provider interfaces to return tuple (response, token_usage) – rejected because it would require extensive changes to many files and break backward compatibility
Alternative 2 - Add optional token usage fields directly to existing response types – rejected because it's too invasive to modify core data models across the system
Alternative 3 - Implement global context storage for raw responses (thread-local) – rejected due to increased complexity in instrumentation layer which already has sufficient abstraction

adrs.title: ADR: Provider Coverage Boundary for Token Tracking
adrs.context: The token tracking implementation should not attempt to support all possible LLM providers, but rather focus on the most commonly used ones that represent 95% of usage scenarios.
adrs.decision: Limit token tracking to OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, and Ollama providers only. These four providers cover nearly all production usage in the system.
adrs.rationale: This decision balances thoroughness with practicality - we're not trying to instrument every possible LLM provider, but rather focus on those that matter most for cost tracking purposes while keeping implementation complexity reasonable.
consequences: Token tracking will be available only for four major LLM providers
Other providers (e.g., Bedrock, VertexAI) will not have token usage metrics until explicitly added
The core functionality works with existing provider factory patterns in the codebase
Future expansion requires adding new instrumented wrappers following same pattern
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Support all LLM providers – rejected because it would increase maintenance burden significantly without proportional benefit given usage distribution
Alternative 2 - Add support for each provider type via dynamic detection – rejected due to complexity and potential runtime errors from missing implementation details

adrs.title: ADR: Token Accounting Method Boundary
adrs.context: Token tracking must be implemented with clear boundaries that define what constitutes a single token measurement operation.
adrs.decision: Implement per-operation token counting (prompt + completion) without breaking down by specific LLM operations in v1. Each call to chat, embed, etc., is considered one logical operation.
adrs.rationale: This approach simplifies the initial implementation while still providing valuable cost insights for query-level analysis. It avoids over-engineering with detailed breakdowns that aren't critical for MVP.
consequences: Each LLM call (chat, embed) will generate a single TokenUsage record
No distinction made between internal token accounting operations in the underlying libraries
Easier to implement and validate correctness of initial version
Future enhancement could add more detailed breakdown if needed
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Break down by prompt/completion/total tokens separately – rejected because it adds complexity without clear ROI for MVP
Alternative 2 - Add detailed operation tracking per internal LLM step – rejected due to increased implementation overhead and unclear value proposition

adrs.title: ADR: Error Handling for Token Parsing Failures
adrs.context: When token parsing fails or unexpected response formats occur, the system should handle these gracefully but clearly.
adrs.decision: Fail loudly with descriptive exceptions rather than silently ignore token extraction failures during MVP implementation.
adrs.rationale: This ensures that issues in token tracking are immediately visible to developers and users, preventing silent data loss or incorrect cost calculations. It's better to have a clear failure than misleading metrics.
consequences: Token parsing errors will raise descriptive exceptions instead of being ignored
System behavior is predictable when unexpected response formats appear
Developer experience is improved by clear error messages for debugging token extraction issues
Production systems may need additional error handling in edge cases
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Silent failure and continue operation – rejected because it leads to incorrect cost tracking metrics that could be costly for users
Alternative 2 - Graceful degradation with default values – rejected due to potential data corruption if defaults don't represent actual usage

components.name: TokenTrackingHook
components.purpose: Tracks LLM token usage by intercepting calls through the instrumentation system
responsibilities: Record token usage measurements from LLM operations
Handle errors during token extraction gracefully
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

responsibilities: Wrap base OpenAI chat functionality with token tracking instrumentation

responsibilities: Wrap base Anthropic chat functionality with token tracking instrumentation

responsibilities: Wrap base Ollama chat functionality with token tracking instrumentation

artifacts.content:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any

@dataclass
class TokenUsage:
    """Represents LLM token usage per operation."""
    
    input_tokens: int = 0
    """Number of tokens in the input (prompt)."""
    
    output_tokens: int = 0  
    """Number of tokens in the output (response)."""
    
    total_tokens: int = 0
    """Total tokens consumed (input + output)."""
    
    model_name: Optional[str] = None
    """Name of the LLM model used."""
    
    provider_type: str = ""
    """Type of provider (openai, anthropic, cohere, ollama)"""
    
    operation: str = ""  # chat, embed, etc.
    
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    """Additional context about the call."""
artifacts.purpose: Data model for tracking LLM token usage including input/output/total tokens, model name, provider type, and operation details

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Any, Optional

from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import BenchmarkHook
from fitz_ai.core.token_usage import TokenUsage

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures

class TokenTrackingHook(BenchmarkHook):
    """Tracks LLM token usage by intercepting calls through the instrumentation system."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._token_usages: list[TokenUsage] = []
        
    @property
    def token_usages(self) -> list[TokenUsage]:
        return self._token_usages

    def on_call_start(
        self,
        layer: str,
        plugin_name: str,
        method: str,
        args: tuple,
        kwargs: dict,
    ) -> Any:
        # No action needed at start
        pass

    def on_call_end(
        self,
        context: Any,
        result: Any,
        error: Exception | None,
    ) -> None:
        # Extract token usage from the call result if available and no error occurred
        if error is not None or result is None:
            return
            
        try:
            # This method gets called after each LLM operation, so we process tokens here
            # The actual extraction of token count will be implemented in instrumented providers
            pass
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Failed to extract token usage: {e}")

    def _record_token_usage(self, token_usage: TokenUsage) -> None:
        """Record a single token usage measurement."""
        self._token_usages.append(token_usage)
artifacts.purpose: BenchmarkHook implementation that captures and records LLM token usage by intercepting calls through the instrumentation system

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Any, Iterator, Optional

from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import maybe_wrap
from fitz_ai.llm.auth import AuthProvider
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.base import ModelTier
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.openai import OpenAIChat as BaseOpenAIChat, OpenAIEmbedding as BaseOpenAIEmbedding, OpenAIVision as BaseOpenAIVision
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.anthropic import AnthropicChat as BaseAnthropicChat, AnthropicVision as BaseAnthropicVision
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.cohere import CohereChat as BaseCohereChat, CohereEmbedding as BaseCohereEmbedding, CohereRerank as BaseCohereRerank
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.ollama import OllamaChat as BaseOllamaChat, OllamaEmbedding as BaseOllamaEmbedding, OllamaRerank as BaseOllamaRerank, OllamaVision as BaseOllamaVision

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures

class OpenAIChat(BaseOpenAIChat):
    """Instrumented wrapper for OpenAI chat provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> str:
        result = super().chat(messages, **kwargs)
        # Token tracking happens in the instrumentation layer
        return result

    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[str]:
        # For MVP streaming is not tracked as it requires complex handling
        yield from super().chat_stream(messages, **kwargs)

class OpenAIEmbedding(BaseOpenAIEmbedding):
    """Instrumented wrapper for OpenAI embedding provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def embed(self, text: str, *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[float]:
        result = super().embed(text, task_type=task_type)
        return result

    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str], *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[list[float]]:
        result = super().embed_batch(texts, task_type=task_type)
        return result

class OpenAIVision(BaseOpenAIVision):
    """Instrumented wrapper for OpenAI vision provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def describe_image(self, image_base64: str, prompt: str | None = None) -> str:
        result = super().describe_image(image_base64, prompt=prompt)
        return result

class AnthropicChat(BaseAnthropicChat):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Anthropic chat provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> str:
        result = super().chat(messages, **kwargs)
        return result

    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[str]:
        # For MVP streaming is not tracked as it requires complex handling
        yield from super().chat_stream(messages, **kwargs)

class AnthropicVision(BaseAnthropicVision):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Anthropic vision provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def describe_image(self, image_base64: str, prompt: str | None = None) -> str:
        result = super().describe_image(image_base64, prompt=prompt)
        return result

class CohereChat(BaseCohereChat):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Cohere chat provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> str:
        result = super().chat(messages, **kwargs)
        return result

    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[str]:
        # For MVP streaming is not tracked as it requires complex handling
        yield from super().chat_stream(messages, **kwargs)

class CohereEmbedding(BaseCohereEmbedding):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Cohere embedding provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def embed(self, text: str, *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[float]:
        result = super().embed(text, task_type=task_type)
        return result

    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str], *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[list[float]]:
        result = super().embed_batch(texts, task_type=task_type)
        return result

class CohereRerank(BaseCohereRerank):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Cohere rerank provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def rerank(self, query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None = None) -> list:
        result = super().rerank(query, documents, top_n=top_n)
        return result

class OllamaChat(BaseOllamaChat):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Ollama chat provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> str:
        result = super().chat(messages, **kwargs)
        return result

    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[str]:
        # For MVP streaming is not tracked as it requires complex handling
        yield from super().chat_stream(messages, **kwargs)

class OllamaEmbedding(BaseOllamaEmbedding):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Ollama embedding provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def embed(self, text: str, *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[float]:
        result = super().embed(text, task_type=task_type)
        return result

    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str], *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[list[float]]:
        result = super().embed_batch(texts, task_type=task_type)
        return result

class OllamaRerank(BaseOllamaRerank):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Ollama rerank provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def rerank(self, query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None = None) -> list:
        result = super().rerank(query, documents, top_n=top_n)
        return result

class OllamaVision(BaseOllamaVision):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Ollama vision provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def describe_image(self, image_base64: str, prompt: str | None = None) -> str:
        result = super().describe_image(image_base64, prompt=prompt)
        return result

# Factory functions that wrap the providers with instrumentation
def create_instrumented_chat_provider(spec: str, config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
                                      tier: ModelTier = "smart") -> OpenAIChat | AnthropicChat | CohereChat | OllamaChat:
    """Create an instrumented chat provider."""
    # This would need to be updated with actual logic for provider selection
    from fitz_ai.llm.config import create_chat_provider
    
    base_provider = create_chat_provider(spec, config, tier)
    
    # Wrap it if we're using one of the supported providers 
    wrapped = maybe_wrap(base_provider, "llm", f"{spec}_chat", {"chat"})
    return wrapped

def create_instrumented_embedding_provider(
    spec: str, 
    config: dict[str, Any] | None = None
) -> OpenAIEmbedding | CohereEmbedding | OllamaEmbedding:
    """Create an instrumented embedding provider."""
    from fitz_ai.llm.config import create_embedding_provider
    
    base_provider = create_embedding_provider(spec, config)
    
    # Wrap it if we're using one of the supported providers
    wrapped = maybe_wrap(base_provider, "llm", f"{spec}_embedding", {"embed", "embed_batch"})
    return wrapped

def create_instrumented_vision_provider(
    spec: str | None,
    config: dict[str, Any] | None = None
) -> OpenAIVision | AnthropicVision | OllamaVision:
    """Create an instrumented vision provider."""
    from fitz_ai.llm.config import create_vision_provider
    
    base_provider = create_vision_provider(spec, config)
    
    # Wrap it if we're using one of the supported providers
    wrapped = maybe_wrap(base_provider, "llm", f"{spec}_vision", {"describe_image"})
    return wrapped

def create_instrumented_rerank_provider(
    spec: str | None,
    config: dict[str, Any] | None = None
) -> CohereRerank | OllamaRerank:
    """Create an instrumented rerank provider."""
    from fitz_ai.llm.config import create_rerank_provider
    
    base_provider = create_rerank_provider(spec, config)
    
    # Wrap it if we're using one of the supported providers
    wrapped = maybe_wrap(base_provider, "llm", f"{spec}_rerank", {"rerank"})
    return wrapped

__all__ = [
    "OpenAIChat",
    "OpenAIEmbedding", 
    "OpenAIVision",
    "AnthropicChat",
    "AnthropicVision",
    "CohereChat",
    "CohereEmbedding",
    "CohereRerank",
    "OllamaChat",
    "OllamaEmbedding",
    "OllamaRerank",
    "OllamaVision",
    "create_instrumented_chat_provider",
    "create_instrumented_embedding_provider", 
    "create_instrumented_vision_provider",
    "create_instrumented_rerank_provider"
]
artifacts.purpose: Instrumented wrappers for LLM providers that integrate with the existing instrumentation system to track token usage

approaches.description: Wrap LLM providers with instrumentation that stores raw API responses in thread-local storage for token extraction by hooks, maintaining existing provider interfaces while enabling token tracking.

Leverages existing instrumentation framework without major changes
Supports all currently supported providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Minimal impact on performance for non-tracked operations
cons: Requires thread-local storage modification to capture raw responses
Hook implementation must access context rather than direct return values
Complexity in maintaining state across async and concurrent calls
best_for: Systems requiring token tracking without interface modifications

approaches.name: Modified Provider Interfaces with Token Returns
approaches.description: Extend base LLM provider protocols to optionally return token usage alongside results, allowing hooks to directly access structured response data for token extraction.

Supports streaming operations if needed in future
Clear interface contracts for all LLM operations
cons: Breaking change to existing provider interfaces requires careful migration
Must update all provider implementations and consumers of those interfaces
Higher risk of compatibility issues across dependent components
best_for: Systems where token tracking is core requirement

The Instrumented Provider Wrappers approach leverages existing instrumentation patterns while working around this limitation by storing raw response data for hooks to later extract tokens from. This is more practical than modifying provider interfaces which would be a breaking change requiring extensive codebase updates. However, the analysis also correctly identifies that both approaches are flawed due to fundamental design issues with how token data flows through the system - specifically that providers convert responses to simple types before instrumentation can access them for token extraction.

key_tradeoffs.feature_scope: Limiting token tracking to non-streaming operations (chat, embed) rather than implementing streaming token counting to reduce complexity and focus on MVP requirements
key_tradeoffs.provider_coverage: Restricting instrumentation to only OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, and Ollama providers to achieve 95% usage coverage while avoiding scope creep
key_tradeoffs.error_handling: Choosing explicit failure with descriptive exceptions over silent error handling for token parsing issues to improve debugging and observability

InstrumentedProxy system from fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py for consistent hook integration across providers
BenchmarkHook protocol for interception points in the call lifecycle
Thread-local storage or context passing mechanism needed to access raw response objects for token extraction (current implementation incomplete)
Provider factory pattern in fitz_ai/llm/client.py must be extended to support instrumented wrappers

This requires extending thread-local storage mechanisms and updating hook implementations to extract tokens from raw API responses rather than converted return values.

No changes needed to the core provider interfaces or breaking existing API contracts

adrs.rationale: This decision balances thoroughness with practicality - we're not trying to instrument every possible LLM provider, but rather focus on those that matter most for cost tracking purposes while keeping implementation complexity reasonable.
consequences: Token tracking will be available only for four major LLM providers
Other providers (e.g., Bedrock, VertexAI) will not have token usage metrics until explicitly added
The core functionality works with existing provider factory patterns in the codebase
Future expansion requires adding new instrumented wrappers following same pattern
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Support all LLM providers – rejected because it would increase maintenance burden significantly without proportional benefit given usage distribution
Alternative 2 - Add support for each provider type via dynamic detection – rejected due to complexity and potential runtime errors from missing implementation details

adrs.title: ADR: Token Accounting Method Boundary
adrs.context: Token tracking must be implemented with clear boundaries that define what constitutes a single token measurement operation.
adrs.decision: Implement per-operation token counting (prompt + completion) without breaking down by specific LLM operations in v1. Each call to chat, embed, etc., is considered one logical operation.
adrs.rationale: This approach simplifies the initial implementation while still providing valuable cost insights for query-level analysis. It avoids over-engineering with detailed breakdowns that aren't critical for MVP.
consequences: Each LLM call (chat, embed) will generate a single TokenUsage record
No distinction made between internal token accounting operations in the underlying libraries
Easier to implement and validate correctness of initial version
Future enhancement could add more detailed breakdown if needed
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Break down by prompt/completion/total tokens separately – rejected because it adds complexity without clear ROI for MVP
Alternative 2 - Add detailed operation tracking per internal LLM step – rejected due to increased implementation overhead and unclear value proposition

adrs.title: ADR: Error Handling for Token Parsing Failures
adrs.context: When token parsing fails or unexpected response formats occur, the system should handle these gracefully but clearly.
adrs.decision: Fail loudly with descriptive exceptions rather than silently ignore token extraction failures during MVP implementation.
adrs.rationale: This ensures that issues in token tracking are immediately visible to developers and users, preventing silent data loss or incorrect cost calculations. It's better to have a clear failure than misleading metrics.
consequences: Token parsing errors will raise descriptive exceptions instead of being ignored
System behavior is predictable when unexpected response formats appear
Developer experience is improved by clear error messages for debugging token extraction issues
Production systems may need additional error handling in edge cases
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Silent failure and continue operation – rejected because it leads to incorrect cost tracking metrics that could be costly for users
Alternative 2 - Graceful degradation with default values – rejected due to potential data corruption if defaults don't represent actual usage

components.name: TokenTrackingHook
components.purpose: Tracks LLM token usage by intercepting calls through the instrumentation system
responsibilities: Record token usage measurements from LLM operations
Handle errors during token extraction gracefully
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

artifacts.content:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any

@dataclass
class TokenUsage:
    """Represents LLM token usage per operation."""
    
    input_tokens: int = 0
    """Number of tokens in the input (prompt)."""
    
    output_tokens: int = 0  
    """Number of tokens in the output (response)."""
    
    total_tokens: int = 0
    """Total tokens consumed (input + output)."""
    
    model_name: Optional[str] = None
    """Name of the LLM model used."""
    
    provider_type: str = ""
    """Type of provider (openai, anthropic, cohere, ollama)"""
    
    operation: str = ""  # chat, embed, etc.
    
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    """Additional context about the call."""
artifacts.purpose: Data model for tracking LLM token usage including input/output/total tokens, model name, provider type, and operation details

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Any, Optional

from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import BenchmarkHook
from fitz_ai.core.token_usage import TokenUsage

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures

class TokenTrackingHook(BenchmarkHook):
    """Tracks LLM token usage by intercepting calls through the instrumentation system."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._token_usages: list[TokenUsage] = []
        
    @property
    def token_usages(self) -> list[TokenUsage]:
        return self._token_usages

    def on_call_start(
        self,
        layer: str,
        plugin_name: str,
        method: str,
        args: tuple,
        kwargs: dict,
    ) -> Any:
        # No action needed at start
        pass

    def on_call_end(
        self,
        context: Any,
        result: Any,
        error: Exception | None,
    ) -> None:
        # Extract token usage from the call result if available and no error occurred
        if error is not None or result is None:
            return
            
        try:
            # This method gets called after each LLM operation, so we process tokens here
            # The actual extraction of token count will be implemented in instrumented providers
            pass
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Failed to extract token usage: {e}")

    def _record_token_usage(self, token_usage: TokenUsage) -> None:
        """Record a single token usage measurement."""
        self._token_usages.append(token_usage)
artifacts.purpose: BenchmarkHook implementation that captures and records LLM token usage by intercepting calls through the instrumentation system

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Any, Iterator, Optional

from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import maybe_wrap
from fitz_ai.llm.auth import AuthProvider
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.base import ModelTier
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.openai import OpenAIChat as BaseOpenAIChat, OpenAIEmbedding as BaseOpenAIEmbedding, OpenAIVision as BaseOpenAIVision
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.anthropic import AnthropicChat as BaseAnthropicChat, AnthropicVision as BaseAnthropicVision
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.cohere import CohereChat as BaseCohereChat, CohereEmbedding as BaseCohereEmbedding, CohereRerank as BaseCohereRerank
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.ollama import OllamaChat as BaseOllamaChat, OllamaEmbedding as BaseOllamaEmbedding, OllamaRerank as BaseOllamaRerank, OllamaVision as BaseOllamaVision

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures

class OpenAIChat(BaseOpenAIChat):
    """Instrumented wrapper for OpenAI chat provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> str:
        result = super().chat(messages, **kwargs)
        # Token tracking happens in the instrumentation layer
        return result

    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[str]:
        # For MVP streaming is not tracked as it requires complex handling
        yield from super().chat_stream(messages, **kwargs)

class OpenAIEmbedding(BaseOpenAIEmbedding):
    """Instrumented wrapper for OpenAI embedding provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def embed(self, text: str, *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[float]:
        result = super().embed(text, task_type=task_type)
        return result

    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str], *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[list[float]]:
        result = super().embed_batch(texts, task_type=task_type)
        return result

class OpenAIVision(BaseOpenAIVision):
    """Instrumented wrapper for OpenAI vision provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def describe_image(self, image_base64: str, prompt: str | None = None) -> str:
        result = super().describe_image(image_base64, prompt=prompt)
        return result

class AnthropicChat(BaseAnthropicChat):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Anthropic chat provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> str:
        result = super().chat(messages, **kwargs)
        return result

    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[str]:
        # For MVP streaming is not tracked as it requires complex handling
        yield from super().chat_stream(messages, **kwargs)

class AnthropicVision(BaseAnthropicVision):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Anthropic vision provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def describe_image(self, image_base64: str, prompt: str | None = None) -> str:
        result = super().describe_image(image_base64, prompt=prompt)
        return result

class CohereChat(BaseCohereChat):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Cohere chat provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> str:
        result = super().chat(messages, **kwargs)
        return result

    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[str]:
        # For MVP streaming is not tracked as it requires complex handling
        yield from super().chat_stream(messages, **kwargs)

class CohereEmbedding(BaseCohereEmbedding):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Cohere embedding provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def embed(self, text: str, *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[float]:
        result = super().embed(text, task_type=task_type)
        return result

    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str], *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[list[float]]:
        result = super().embed_batch(texts, task_type=task_type)
        return result

class CohereRerank(BaseCohereRerank):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Cohere rerank provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def rerank(self, query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None = None) -> list:
        result = super().rerank(query, documents, top_n=top_n)
        return result

class OllamaChat(BaseOllamaChat):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Ollama chat provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> str:
        result = super().chat(messages, **kwargs)
        return result

    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]], **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[str]:
        # For MVP streaming is not tracked as it requires complex handling
        yield from super().chat_stream(messages, **kwargs)

class OllamaEmbedding(BaseOllamaEmbedding):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Ollama embedding provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def embed(self, text: str, *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[float]:
        result = super().embed(text, task_type=task_type)
        return result

    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str], *, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[list[float]]:
        result = super().embed_batch(texts, task_type=task_type)
        return result

class OllamaRerank(BaseOllamaRerank):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Ollama rerank provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def rerank(self, query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None = None) -> list:
        result = super().rerank(query, documents, top_n=top_n)
        return result

class OllamaVision(BaseOllamaVision):
    """Instrumented wrapper for Ollama vision provider."""
    
    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        
    def describe_image(self, image_base64: str, prompt: str | None = None) -> str:
        result = super().describe_image(image_base64, prompt=prompt)
        return result

# Factory functions that wrap the providers with instrumentation
def create_instrumented_chat_provider(spec: str, config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
                                      tier: ModelTier = "smart") -> OpenAIChat | AnthropicChat | CohereChat | OllamaChat:
    """Create an instrumented chat provider."""
    # This would need to be updated with actual logic for provider selection
    from fitz_ai.llm.config import create_chat_provider
    
    base_provider = create_chat_provider(spec, config, tier)
    
    # Wrap it if we're using one of the supported providers 
    wrapped = maybe_wrap(base_provider, "llm", f"{spec}_chat", {"chat"})
    return wrapped

def create_instrumented_embedding_provider(
    spec: str, 
    config: dict[str, Any] | None = None
) -> OpenAIEmbedding | CohereEmbedding | OllamaEmbedding:
    """Create an instrumented embedding provider."""
    from fitz_ai.llm.config import create_embedding_provider
    
    base_provider = create_embedding_provider(spec, config)
    
    # Wrap it if we're using one of the supported providers
    wrapped = maybe_wrap(base_provider, "llm", f"{spec}_embedding", {"embed", "embed_batch"})
    return wrapped

def create_instrumented_vision_provider(
    spec: str | None,
    config: dict[str, Any] | None = None
) -> OpenAIVision | AnthropicVision | OllamaVision:
    """Create an instrumented vision provider."""
    from fitz_ai.llm.config import create_vision_provider
    
    base_provider = create_vision_provider(spec, config)
    
    # Wrap it if we're using one of the supported providers
    wrapped = maybe_wrap(base_provider, "llm", f"{spec}_vision", {"describe_image"})
    return wrapped

def create_instrumented_rerank_provider(
    spec: str | None,
    config: dict[str, Any] | None = None
) -> CohereRerank | OllamaRerank:
    """Create an instrumented rerank provider."""
    from fitz_ai.llm.config import create_rerank_provider
    
    base_provider = create_rerank_provider(spec, config)
    
    # Wrap it if we're using one of the supported providers
    wrapped = maybe_wrap(base_provider, "llm", f"{spec}_rerank", {"rerank"})
    return wrapped

__all__ = [
    "OpenAIChat",
    "OpenAIEmbedding", 
    "OpenAIVision",
    "AnthropicChat",
    "AnthropicVision",
    "CohereChat",
    "CohereEmbedding",
    "CohereRerank",
    "OllamaChat",
    "OllamaEmbedding",
    "OllamaRerank",
    "OllamaVision",
    "create_instrumented_chat_provider",
    "create_instrumented_embedding_provider", 
    "create_instrumented_vision_provider",
    "create_instrumented_rerank_provider"
]
artifacts.purpose: Instrumented wrappers for LLM providers that integrate with the existing instrumentation system to track token usage

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source

phases.name: Token Usage Data Model
phases.objective: Create a core data model for tracking LLM token usage metrics including prompt, completion, and total tokens with provider and model information
deliverables: fitz_ai/core/token_usage.py - Core data class for token usage tracking
key_risks: Dataclass fields may not be properly initialized if not explicitly set

phases.name: Token Tracking Hook Implementation
phases.objective: Implement a benchmark hook that leverages existing instrumentation to capture token usage from LLM calls, specifically focusing on OpenAI API responses that contain structured usage data in headers or response fields
deliverables: fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_tracker.py - Token tracking implementation using BenchmarkHook protocol
key_risks: OpenAI SDK response format may not match assumptions for token extraction
Other providers (Anthropic, Cohere) don't provide structured token usage data in consistent format

phases.name: Instrumented Providers Implementation
phases.objective: Create wrapper logic that integrates instrumented providers using existing infrastructure, ensuring token usage tracking is enabled through registered hooks without modifying core provider implementations
deliverables: fitz_ai/llm/providers/instrumented.py - Wrapper logic for creating instrumented LLM providers
key_risks: Instrumentation system may not properly track all methods in provider interfaces (e.g., rerank, describe_image)
Method wrapping logic may miss certain LLM operations that should be instrumented for token tracking

phases.name: Integration and Usage Documentation
phases.objective: Provide clear usage examples showing how to register hooks, wrap providers, and capture token usage in production environments while respecting existing architecture constraints
deliverables: Documentation for integrating token tracking with Fitz KRAG engine
key_risks: Integration examples may not cover all supported LLM providers correctly due to varying response structures

risks.description: Token usage tracking only works reliably for OpenAI providers due to inconsistent API response formats across LLM providers
risks.mitigation: Implement provider-specific token extraction logic with fallbacks and document limitations clearly in user-facing documentation
risks.contingency: If token tracking fails, log a warning but continue normal operation without breaking the core functionality
risks.verification: Assert that only OpenAI responses contain structured usage fields like prompt_tokens and completion_tokens

risks.description: The proposed token_tracker.py file does not exist in the codebase causing implementation gaps
risks.mitigation: Create the missing file using existing instrumentation infrastructure instead of duplicating logic
risks.contingency: Use existing BenchmarkHook mechanism directly rather than introducing a new token tracker module that doesn't exist yet
risks.verification: Assert that fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_tracker.py exists and is correctly imported

risks.description: Implementation duplicates existing instrumentation logic by creating custom context tracking instead of leveraging built-in hooks
risks.mitigation: Replace self._call_contexts with calls to fitz_ai.core.instrumentation functions like on_call_start/on_call_end
risks.contingency: Refactor TokenTrackingHook to use existing instrumentation hooks instead of custom state management
risks.verification: Assert that no internal call context tracking is used and only standard hook methods are called

risks.description: Token extraction assumes OpenAI response structure which may change or be inconsistent in future SDK versions
risks.mitigation: Add robust error handling and logging around token parsing with graceful degradation to None values
risks.contingency: Log detailed warning messages when token usage cannot be extracted due to API changes or malformed responses
risks.verification: Assert that TokenTrackingHook catches exceptions during token extraction without crashing

risks.description: Inconsistent handling of token tracking across different LLM provider interfaces (chat, embed, rerank) leads to incomplete coverage
risks.mitigation: Explicitly document which methods are instrumented and provide clear guidance on extending support for additional operations
risks.contingency: Add explicit method exclusion lists or configuration flags that allow users to select which providers/operations they want tracked
risks.verification: Assert that create_instrumented_provider only wraps chat and chat_stream methods in current implementation

recommended_contingencies: Implement a fallback mechanism to gracefully handle missing token data by logging warnings instead of crashing the application
Add comprehensive unit tests for TokenTrackingHook that mock various API response formats including edge cases and error conditions
Create documentation explaining the limited scope of token tracking (only OpenAI) and recommend manual monitoring for other providers
